screenshots.status: SUCCESS — page loads cleanly, no console errors, vibe at capture: Crushing It

carry_over_resolved.setTimeout_memory_leak_ambient_broadcast: FIXED ✅ — Carlos's fix is confirmed. The ambient broadcast useEffect in Character (lines 526–561) properly clears both mountQuoteId (clearTimeout) and the 45s interval (clearInterval) in its cleanup. No dangling timers from the main loop.
carry_over_resolved.office_jsx_split: NOT FIXED ❌ [3 REVIEWS] — still exactly 1515 lines.
carry_over_resolved.fragmented_media_queries: NOT FIXED ❌ [3 REVIEWS] — Office.css has THREE separate @media (max-width: 480px) blocks (lines 1133, 2165, 2316) and ELEVEN @media (prefers-reduced-motion) blocks.
carry_over_resolved.lone_survivor_label_inline_styles: NOT FIXED ❌ [3 REVIEWS] — Office.jsx line 1419 still has a 7-property inline style object.
carry_over_resolved.vibe_weather_constants_in_utils: NOT FIXED ❌ [3 REVIEWS] — VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS and VIBE_WEATHER_ICONS are still module-level constants in Office.jsx (lines 108–121), not in utils/constants.js.

maya_work_status.ficus_streak_tooltip: PARTIALLY DEPLOYED — The ficus renders correctly and the plantToast fires on stage up. However, there is no custom CSS tooltip component on hover; it relies solely on the native `title` attribute (Office.jsx line 1456). The ficus div is `aria-hidden='true'`, making the streak info inaccessible to screen reader and keyboard users. The streak info exists only via mouse hover on desktop.
maya_work_status.ambient_particle_vibe_color: APPEARS WORKING — Office-floor particles (Office.jsx line 1501) have no `data-vibe` on their container, but they correctly inherit vibe color via the parent `.office-floor[data-vibe=X]` rule. App-level particles correctly use `data-vibe={teamVibe.key}` on their container. No visual regression detected.

visual_observations.desktop: Dark theme polished, all 5 workers at desks, Bloberto at CVO desk with 🏆 trophy. Speech bubble ('was special.') is clipped at the top — overflows above the office-floor boundary. COFFEE TRIPLE SHOT in coffee corner. Decorative plants, cactus, ficus visible. Shortcut toast showing at page load (correct, first visit). Conference table lower-left. Whiteboard shows SHIP IT with 0% avg progress.
visual_observations.mobile: Layout holds. All 5 workers legible. Office floor scales acceptably. 'tap / clack / click' keystroke labels on active desks are very small but present. Header wraps cleanly. Footer visible at bottom.

suggestions.title: Inner setTimeouts inside interval callbacks not cleaned up on unmount
suggestions.description: In Character component, the idle bubble effect (Office.jsx lines 486–494) and error bubble effect (lines 503–511) each call setTimeout() inside both their setInterval callback AND their initialTimeout callback. The cleanup only calls clearInterval(interval) and clearTimeout(initialTimeout) — it does NOT track or cancel the inner `setTimeout(() => setIdleBubble(false), 2200)` and `setTimeout(() => setErrorBubble(null), 2200)` calls. If a Character unmounts while one of these inner timeouts is in flight (e.g., worker goes offline mid-animation), the timer fires after unmount and attempts a setState on an unmounted component. Fix: track inner timers in a ref (e.g. innerTimerRef) and clear them in the cleanup function alongside the outer ones.

suggestions.title: Bloberto's speech bubble clipped by office-floor overflow:hidden
suggestions.description: Bloberto is placed at `top={4}%` inside `.office-floor` (Office.jsx line 1401). The `.speech-bubble` is positioned `bottom: calc(100% + 8px)` above the character. At 4% from the top of the container, the bubble does not fit and is guillotined by `overflow: hidden` on the office-floor. Visible in desktop screenshot — the bubble reads 'was special.' with the top cut off. Fixes: (a) give `.office-floor` a top padding/clip-aware margin, (b) add `overflow: visible` scoped to the manager character area, or (c) increase Bloberto's `top` prop to ~12% and adjust mgr-desk positioning accordingly.

suggestions.title: [3 REVIEWS] Office.jsx at 1515 lines — must be split
suggestions.description: Office.jsx has not been reduced in size across three consecutive reviews. At 1515 lines it contains: SVG constants (DESK_SCREEN_SVG, ~100 lines), all character SVG components (CharacterAvatar, ~370 lines), UI components (Character, AwaySign, WindowElement, ConferenceTable, WallClock, VibeShockwave — ~550 lines), local constants (BLOBERTO_TITLES, VIBE_WEATHER_*, LONE_SURVIVOR_LABELS, VACANT_ADS, PING_REACTIONS, VIBE_COLORS, VIBE_RANK, VIBE_SHOCKWAVE_COLORS), and the main Office component. Recommended split: src/components/CharacterAvatar.jsx, src/components/OfficeDecor.jsx, src/components/DeskComponents.jsx. This is a blocking technical debt item now three reviews old — escalate to sprint blocker.

suggestions.title: [3 REVIEWS] Fragmented @media blocks — 11 prefers-reduced-motion + 3 mobile breakpoints in Office.css
suggestions.description: Office.css has three separate `@media (max-width: 480px)` blocks at lines 1133, 2165, and 2316. More critically, it has ELEVEN separate `@media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce)` blocks scattered throughout the file (lines 2, 714, 1068, 1454, 1577, 1614, 1654, 1691, 1701, 1996, 2033). App.css has seven more prefers-reduced-motion blocks. This fragmentation makes accessibility overrides unreliable and nearly impossible to audit. Each breakpoint should have ONE consolidated block at the bottom of each file. Total: 18 prefers-reduced-motion fragments that should be 2 (one per file).

suggestions.title: [3 REVIEWS] VIBE_WEATHER constants still in Office.jsx, not in utils
suggestions.description: VIBE_WEATHER_LABELS (lines 108–114) and VIBE_WEATHER_ICONS (lines 115–121) are defined as module-level constants in Office.jsx despite utils/constants.js existing and already exporting VIBE_WHITEBOARD. Additionally, BLOBERTO_TITLES (lines 100–106), LONE_SURVIVOR_LABELS (lines 123–129), VACANT_ADS (lines 131–137), PING_REACTIONS (lines 139–145), VIBE_COLORS (lines 344–350), VIBE_RANK (line 351), and VIBE_SHOCKWAVE_COLORS (lines 914–920) are all candidates for extraction. At minimum VIBE_WEATHER_* should move to utils/constants.js as requested since review #31.

suggestions.title: [3 REVIEWS] lone-survivor-label div still has 7-property inline style object
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1419: `<div className='lone-survivor-label' style={{ fontSize: '0.45rem', opacity: 0.6, position: 'absolute', bottom: '-0.6rem', left: '50%', transform: 'translateX(-50%)', whiteSpace: 'nowrap', color: 'rgba(255,255,255,0.7)' }}>`. This should be a CSS class `.lone-survivor-label` in Office.css with all seven properties moved there. Three reviews of asking.

suggestions.title: plantToast and completionToast use large inline style objects
suggestions.description: Office.jsx lines 1166–1189 (plantToast) and App.jsx lines 1052–1073 (completionToast) both use large inline style objects with ~10 CSS properties each (position, transform, background, border, borderRadius, padding, fontSize, fontWeight, zIndex, etc.). These are essentially duplicate toast styles. Create a shared `.toast-popup` CSS class in App.css and a `.toast-popup--green` / `.toast-popup--role-color` modifier. The dynamic border color can remain as an inline style or CSS variable override.

suggestions.title: Ficus streak tooltip inaccessible to keyboard and screen reader users
suggestions.description: The ficus element (Office.jsx lines 1452–1462) is `aria-hidden='true'` (intentional, it's decorative) BUT the streak information is only surfaced via `title` attribute — which is not reliably exposed by screen readers and is completely invisible to keyboard-only users who can't hover. The vibe streak is a game mechanic meaningful to sighted users. Two options: (1) Keep ficus aria-hidden, but add a visible sr-only text element nearby that announces the streak (e.g., `Vibe streak: {vibeStreak} — {plantStageLabel}`), or (2) add a proper custom tooltip component (which is what Maya seems to be working on). The `plantToast` on stage-up is good but doesn't convey current streak state.

suggestions.title: WorkerCard uses role='article' — semantically incorrect
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 261: `role='article'` is set on each WorkerCard div. The WAI-ARIA spec describes `article` as 'A section of a page that consists of a composition that forms an independent part of a document, page, or site.' Each WorkerCard is a widget, not an article. A more appropriate role would be `role='listitem'` (if the grid is a `role='list'`) or `role='group'` with `aria-labelledby` pointing to the worker name. Change WorkerCard's role to `group` and wrap the worker grid in a `role='list'` container.

suggestions.title: Task tag spans use role='button' but are not focusable by default without tabIndex
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 295–302: task tags conditionally get `role='button'` and `tabIndex={0}` when `onTagClick` is defined, but the `onKeyDown` handler is only wired when `onTagClick` is truthy. This is correct, but the pattern of using `<span role='button'>` instead of `<button>` means native button semantics (activation on Space/Enter via UA, implicit accessibility tree button type) are not inherited. These should be `<button type='button'>` elements styled to look like tags. Currently keyboard users CAN activate them (Enter/Space), but it requires the manual onKeyDown implementation.

suggestions.title: Tab title cycling creates stale timer if visibility toggles rapidly
suggestions.description: App.jsx lines 962–1008: The tab title effect uses a mutable `let timer` in the closure, and the `handleVisibilityChange` callback both clears AND recreates `timer`. If `visibilitychange` fires `visible → hidden → visible` rapidly, two separate `setInterval` calls could coexist briefly before the second `clearInterval(timer)` runs (since `timer` is reassigned, but the reference in the closure changes). The result is two simultaneous tab title cycling intervals. Fix: use `timerRef.current` (a ref) instead of `let timer` to ensure the cleanup always cancels the correct interval regardless of reassignments.

suggestions.title: VIBE_COLORS defined twice — in Office.jsx and App.css has the same hex values
suggestions.description: Office.jsx lines 344–350 defines `VIBE_COLORS` with vibe-to-hex mappings. The same hex values appear in App.css lines 1035–1039 as inline values. Additionally, ROLE_COLORS in utils/constants.js and VIBE_SHOCKWAVE_COLORS in Office.jsx (lines 914–920) have overlapping color palettes. Recommend: extract a single source of truth as CSS custom properties (e.g., `--vibe-crushing: #a78bfa`) on `:root` and reference them in both JSX (via `getComputedStyle`) and CSS. This eliminates hex duplication across 3+ locations.

suggestions.title: ShortcutToast shows on every fresh session even on mobile where shortcuts don't apply
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 548-601: ShortcutToast checks `localStorage.getItem('bloberto-shortcuts-shown')` and shows keyboard shortcut hints on first visit. App.css line 1102 has `@media (pointer: coarse) { .shortcut-toast { display: none !important; } }` — this suppresses it on touch devices. BUT the toast still mounts, sets timers, and attempts to show. The phase machine goes through 'pending' → 'visible' then the CSS hides it visually, but the localStorage is still written as 'shown'. On desktop after first dismissal this is fine, but on coarse-pointer devices the toast goes through 2 timers (showTimer 1500ms, autoTimer 7000ms) purely to be hidden by CSS. Add a media query check at initialization to skip the toast entirely on touch devices.

suggestions.title: stuckWorkers card shows worker emoji that doesn't exist on most workers
suggestions.description: App.jsx line 477: `stuckWorkers[0].emoji` — the emoji field only exists on roster members that explicitly define it (e.g., luna in DEFAULT_ROSTER). Most workers loaded from the API or from DEFAULT_ROSTER don't have an emoji field, so this renders as `undefined ${name}`. Should fall back to `getRoleEmoji(stuckWorkers[0].role)` or use the character's avatar emoji from ROLE_EMOJIS.

suggestions.title: Mobile: office-floor shortcut toast wraps awkwardly at 375px
suggestions.description: At 375px viewport width, the shortcut toast text '⌨️ Shortcuts: 1 Office · 2 Dashboard · R Refresh · Esc Clear' is too wide and wraps in an ungainly way. App.css line 1105 has some 480px responsive styles but the toast text itself isn't shortened. Consider: abbreviate to '⌨️ 1=Office 2=Dashboard R=Refresh' or add a `max-width` with ellipsis. Note that @media (pointer: coarse) already hides this entirely (correctly), so this only affects edge cases where coarse pointer isn't detected.

suggestions.title: DESK_SCREEN_SVG is 98 lines of JSX defined at module level in Office.jsx
suggestions.description: Office.jsx lines 8–98 define DESK_SCREEN_SVG as a static object containing JSX elements. Since it's static (never changes at runtime), this is fine logically, but it contributes significantly to the 1515-line file length. When Office.jsx is split (see the large split task), DESK_SCREEN_SVG should move to its own file src/data/deskScreens.jsx or be co-located with a new DeskScreen component.

suggestions.title: office-floor region aria-label is very verbose and redundant
suggestions.description: Office.jsx line 1192: `aria-label='Virtual office visualization showing team members at desks'` on the office-floor div. This is fine but could be simplified to `aria-label='Office floor'` since the surrounding `role='region'` and tab panel structure already provide context. The current label will cause screen readers to announce the full string on every focus, which may be annoying for power users. Also, `aria-live` regions inside this (vibeCaption, full-sync-banner, etc.) work correctly.

whats_good: Carlos's ambient broadcast setTimeout fix confirmed working — cleanup in Character useEffect (lines 557–560) is solid.
No console errors whatsoever on load.
Dynamic favicon implementation is clever and works correctly.
Tab title cycling with worker status frames is a delightful detail.
Polling pause on inactivity with visual indicator (polling-paused-pill) is a genuinely useful feature.
Vibe sparkline history in the stats bar is a great visual added feature.
Worker card flip animation for activity history is smooth and well-implemented.
The character SVGs (Carlos with glasses, Maya's pink/purple hair, Dave with headphones, Sofia with magnifying glass, Luna with crescent moon) are all charming and unique.
Speech bubble overflow/hide on mouseLeave is handled cleanly with ref tracking to prevent ambient overlap.
ARIA live regions are properly placed on dynamic content (completion toasts, vibe captions, sync status).
Hash-based tab navigation syncs with browser back/forward — underrated UX detail.
The shortcut toast has proper phase state machine with gotit feedback and localStorage memory.
The ficus growth mechanic with milestone toast on stage-up is working end-to-end.
